MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA, FOR A PROTECTIVE AND RESTRAINING ORDER, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. ******REDACTED***** (TRANSACTION ID # 62171996) FILED BY PLAINTIFF AALAEI, ALI A. AN INDIVIDUAL ARI LAW, P.C. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS ALI AALAEI AND ARI LAW, P.C'S MOTION TO SEAL COMPLAINT AND RELATED FILING - PLAINTIFFS' MOTION "TO SEAL COMPLAINT AND RELATED FILINGS" IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFFS FILED THEIR COMPLAINT IN UNREDACTED FORM BACK ON APRIL 30. FOR THE EIGHT-PLUS WEEKS SINCE THEN, THE COMPLAINT HAS BEEN PUBLICALLY AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S REGISTER OF ACTIONS - APPARENTLY WAIVING ANY CONFIDENTIALITY THAT COULD EVER HAVE EXISTED. FURTHER, MY REVIEW OF THE PUBLIC COMPLAINT FINDS NO IMMEDIATELY APPARENT "OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD." (SEE CRC 2550.) I ALSO AGREE WITH DEFENDANT'S CONCERN THAT GRANTING THE PENDING MOTION WOULD GIVE PLAINTIFFS "A BLANK CHECK TO UNILATERALLY FILE UNDER SEAL ANY FUTURE FILING OF THEIR AND THEIR COUNSEL'S CHOOSING IF THEY, IN THEIR SOLE DISCRETION, BELIEVE IT SHOULD BE SHIELDED FROM PUBLIC VIEW." ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220 =302/RU

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena, For A Protective And Restraining Order, And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-10-2018 Continued To Aug-31-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Complaint And Related Filing; Memorandum Of Points And Autorities; Declaration Of Babak Hashemi, Esq.
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Babak Hashemi, Tel. No. 1-949-464-8529 appearing for the Plaintiffs by
Telephone appearance;
Atty. Matthew S. Kahn of Gibson Dunn LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-393-8200 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS ALI AALAEI and ARI LAW, P.C's Motion To Seal Complaint And Related Filing - Plaintiffs' motion "to seal complaint and related filings" is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Plaintiffs filed their complaint in unredacted form back on April 30. For the eight-plus weeks since then, the complaint has been publically available on the Court's register of actions - apparently waiving any confidentiality that could ever have existed. Further, my review of the public complaint finds no immediately apparent "overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record." (See CRC 2550.) I also agree with defendant's concern that granting the pending motion would give plaintiffs "a blank check to unilaterally file under seal any future filing of their and their counsel's choosing if they, in their sole discretion, believe it should be shielded from public view." Order signed. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR #5220 =302/ru

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Complaint And Related Filing; Memorandum Of Points And Autorities; Declaration Of Babak Hashemi, Esq.

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFS ALI AALAEI and ARI LAW, P.C's Motion To Seal Complaint is continued to June 27, 2018 to be heard by Judge Ulmer. Judge Kahn has recused himself from this case. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =(302/HEK)
